summary: The labeling taxonomy, prompt contract, heuristic signals, and fallback behavior are locked and parsable as JSON-ready contracts.

checks.name: CHECK-01: Prompt file JSON-only
checks.expected: Prompt exists and enforces JSON-only output
checks.actual: Prompt starts with CONTENT_LABELING and explicitly says Return valid JSON only. No markdown fences.

checks.name: CHECK-02: Taxonomy constants match docs
checks.expected: author_role, content_intent, commerciality, relevance, source, audience presets defined
checks.actual: label_taxonomy.py defines AUTHOR_ROLES, CONTENT_INTENTS, COMMERCIALITY_LEVELS, USER_FEEDBACK_RELEVANCE_LEVELS, LABEL_SOURCES, LABEL_JOB_STATUSES, LABEL_RECORD_STATUSES, AUDIENCE_FILTERS and TAXONOMY_VERSION=v1

checks.name: CHECK-03: Heuristic markers
checks.actual: Heuristics include SELLER_PATTERNS, OFFICIAL_PATTERNS, ADMIN_PATTERNS, QUESTION_PATTERNS, EXPERIENCE_PATTERNS, SUPPORT_PATTERNS, and COMPARISON_PATTERNS

checks.name: CHECK-04: Fallback rules documented in code
checks.expected: Fallback path is explicit and non-silent
checks.actual: fallback_label() returns a fallback payload and ContentLabelingService uses ai_missing_record_output when AI omits a record

checks.name: CHECK-05: Prompt sample parses to JSON schema
checks.expected: records[] JSON payload with taxonomy fields
checks.actual: Mock AIClient returned {records:[{post_id:'p1', author_role:'end_user', content_intent:'question', commerciality_level:'low', user_feedback_relevance:'high', label_confidence:0.78, label_source:'ai', taxonomy_version:'v1'}]}
